# File Ownership

## Why Ownership Matters

Most regressions in this repository come from the wrong layer writing the right file.

Examples:

- a repo file shadows an Omarchy template unexpectedly
- a theme script overwrites Waybar live files that should remain local runtime state
- a repair flow copies from a dirty Omarchy source tree instead of a clean git `HEAD`

This document defines the ownership contract.

## Ownership Categories

### Repo-owned generated files

These files are versioned in the repository and regenerated by `scripts/build-theme.sh`.

| File | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `colors.toml` | Template input for Omarchy-generated surfaces |
| `colors.fish` | Fish shell colors |
| `fzf.fish` | fzf colors |
| `waybar.css` | Theme-owned Waybar color layer |
| `walker.css` | Walker styling |
| `gtk.css` | GTK theme variables |
| `hyprland.conf` | Hyprland visual config |
| `starship.toml` | Prompt config |
| `qbar.css` | Future-facing qbar contract |
| `steam.css` | Steam theme variables |
| `vencord.theme.css` | Vencord/Discord theme variables |
| `neovim.lua` | Neovim theme integration |
| `cava_theme` | Cava theme |
| `lazygit.yml` | Lazygit theme |

These should be treated as generated outputs. Edit `theme.tokens.sh`, not the generated file, unless you are intentionally changing the generator itself.

### Repo-owned static files

These files are versioned but not generated.

| File | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `theme.tokens.sh` | Single source of truth for tokens |
| `vscode.json` | VSCode/Codium/Cursor theme selector |
| `icons.theme` | GNOME icon theme name |
| `preview.png` | Preview image |
| `backgrounds/*` | Wallpaper assets |
| `scripts/*` | Operational entrypoints |
| `docs/*` | Documentation |

### Template-driven runtime files

These should not exist as root-level repo files unless the theme intentionally takes ownership away from Omarchy templates.

| Runtime file | Source |
| --- | --- |
| `alacritty.toml` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `kitty.conf` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `ghostty.conf` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `hyprlock.conf` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `mako.ini` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `swayosd.css` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `btop.theme` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `chromium.theme` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `obsidian.css` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |
| `keyboard.rgb` | Omarchy template from `colors.toml` |

## Runtime Ownership Matrix

| Surface | Live runtime path | Owned by | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Active theme overlay | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/*` | Omarchy theme application | Omarchy copies the theme here on `omarchy-theme-set` |
| Waybar live symlinks | `~/.config/waybar/config.jsonc`, `~/.config/waybar/style.css` | Local runtime repair flow | Repointed by `repair-waybar.sh` |
| Waybar local stock base | `~/.config/waybar/themes/omarchy-default/*` | Local runtime repair flow | Built from Omarchy git `HEAD` |
| Waybar color layer | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/waybar.css` | This theme | Colors only |
| Walker CSS | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/walker.css` | This theme | Direct output |
| Hyprland theme file | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/hyprland.conf` | This theme | Direct output |
| Terminal themes | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/{alacritty.toml,kitty.conf,ghostty.conf}` | Omarchy templates | Generated from `colors.toml` |
| Hyprlock theme | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/hyprlock.conf` | Omarchy templates | Generated from `colors.toml` |
| Mako/SwayOSD/btop | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/*` | Omarchy templates | Generated from `colors.toml` |
| GNOME icon theme | `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme/icons.theme` | This repo static file | Consumed by `omarchy-theme-set-gnome` |
| qbar integration | user-local qbar files and modules | qbar | Explicitly not owned by this theme |
| Omarchy source files | `~/.local/share/omarchy/*` | Omarchy upstream/source repo | Read-only for this project |

## Forbidden Mutation Zones

These paths must not be edited by this theme repository or its helper scripts:

- `~/.local/share/omarchy/config/waybar/*`
- anything else under `~/.local/share/omarchy/`

Reading from that tree is allowed and expected. Writing to it is not.

## Allowed Mutation Zones

These are the intended writable targets of the operational scripts:

- `~/.config/omarchy/themes/flat-onedark`
- `~/.config/omarchy/current/theme`
- `~/.config/starship.toml`
- `~/.config/waybar/themes/omarchy-default`
- `~/.config/waybar/config.jsonc`
- `~/.config/waybar/style.css`

## Legacy Forbidden Pattern

These paths must not come back:

- `waybar-theme/config.jsonc`
- `waybar-theme/style.css`

Reason:

- they encourage the repo to own Waybar layout and wrapper again
- they were part of the chain that created cross-theme drift and inconsistent “defaults”

## qbar Boundary

qbar is intentionally outside the ownership boundary of the default bar.

This repo may publish:

- `qbar.css`

This repo must not publish:

- qbar modules inside the repaired stock Waybar layout
- qbar icons inside the default Waybar contract
- qbar-specific live Waybar wiring

## Maintenance Rule

If a surface looks wrong, decide ownership before changing code.

Ask:

1. Is this surface repo-owned?
2. Is it template-driven?
3. Is it local runtime state?
4. Is it actually qbar or another tool outside this theme?

Do not patch symptoms until the owner is identified.
